nodes.moveReason: Jump the knight to the active c6 outpost with check, forcing Black to stay on the back rank while White starts building the handoff cage.
Use the forcing knight check to enter the longer preparation route; from d5 the knight can pivot through f4 and g6 while the king holds the center.
Centralize the knight to d4 so it can reach c6 or the f5/e2 transfer squares without dropping the king or bishop below the fourth rank.
Move into opposition on d6, taking the key square beside the knight so Black cannot use the d-file to loosen the cage.
Take d6 to keep the knight protected and to prepare a direct finish if Black stays on the back rank.
Retreat the knight to f4 to preserve the bishop color parity and start the g6 triangulation route against Black's f8 defense.
Bring the king to d6, using the knight on d4 as a shield while White takes the opposition squares around d8.
Send the knight to c6 to check the king's escape squares and reconnect with the standard prepared pattern.
Use the king move to f6 to cut off e7 and g7, making the knight transfer to c6 or f5 safe.
Transfer the knight through e2 so it can return to f4 with the right color and without blocking the king on d6.
Put the knight back on c6, restoring the familiar cage with the king on f6 ready to finish.
This is the direct handoff: the knight reaches f7, side-adjacent to both kings and matching the bishop's square color.
Bring the king to d6 so the advanced knight on g6 has support and Black cannot escape through e7.
Take d6 to hold the center; with the knight already on g6, White only needs to convert the edge cage.
Bring the knight back to f4, restoring the working color and setting up the final g6 or e2 route.
Use f4 as the knight's transfer square; it keeps Black boxed while avoiding a premature wrong-color finish.
Move the knight to g6, tightening the cage before the king finishes the opposition pattern.
Step to c6 to triangulate from the d-file and force Black back into the final Nd7 handoff.
Move to f6, keeping the h8 king on the edge and preserving the knight's direct route to g6.
Return to f6 to hold the h6 king in place and avoid giving up the edge cage.
Finish with Nd7: the knight becomes side-adjacent to both kings on the bishop's color.
Move the king to f6 so the knight on g6 and bishop on e6 lock the final h-file cage.
